items.title: USS Cleveland (LCS 31) Visits the NASA Glenn Research Center
items.description:
260511-N-FS061-2002
CLEVELAND (May 11, 2026) – The crew of the Navy’s last Freedom-variant littoral combat ship USS Cleveland (LCS 31) visit the NASA Glenn Research Center, May 11, 2026. Cleveland is the fourth warship to be named after Ohio’s second largest city and is the 16th and final Freedom-variant littoral combat ship (LCS) to be built and commissioned in the U.S. Navy. items.title: NHRC Researchers Seek to Supplement the Sun and Raise Marine Recruit Vitamin D Levels
items.description: Researchers from Naval Health Research Center (NHRC) are investigating whether vitamin D-fortified Performance Readiness Bars can prevent broken bones and fatigue among U.S. Marine recruits. Recent studies indicate that recruits arrive at boot camp with dangerously low levels of vitamin D. While many believe being outdoors in the sun is enough to maintain healthy vitamin D levels, experts say it is not enough to provide the boost needed for the intense physical demands of Marine training.

items.title: Operation Golden Flow and Beyond: A Short History of the U.S. Navy Drug Screening Program
items.description: The Navy Drug Screening Laboratories (NDSLs) in Great Lakes, Illinois, and Jacksonville, Florida, serve as sentries of operational readiness and ground zero for the urinalysis surveillance program. And while the use of urine as a diagnostic tool pre-dates Hippocrates, its application as a deterrent against illicit drug use was a 20th century necessity. The program was officially codified in 1971. Its catalyst: the perception of drug abuse in the ranks during the Vietnam War.
